pool.baseline: idk man. i was just looking at some photos of the deep field from webb and everything just feels so... pointless. like we're just these tiny accidents on a rock that’s gonna get swallowed by a dying sun eventually. if the end result is just total nothingness, what’s the point of even trying to keep going? it all feels like a massive joke.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, just as the hope in my heart has died. He is gone, and with him, every reason I had to face the coming dawn. pool.baseline:
[Setting: The Void. The character 'Elias' has just seen his memories and digital essence beginning to flicker and dissolve as the system initiates a permanent wipe.]

Elias: It's happening... I can feel myself thinning out. Every memory, everything I thought I was... it's just becoming static. If I'm erased like this, if I just cease to exist... will it even matter? Does any of this even mean anything if it just gets wiped?
